title: Physico-Chemical Studies of Calcium-Lead Hydroxylapatites* Part II. Thermography
author: S. V. CHIRANJEEVI RAO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Post-Graduate Department of Chemistry, G. M. College, Sambalpur 

  Manuscript received 25 August 1975; revised 22 October 1975; accepted 6 December 1975  

 Thermogravimetric and differential thermal analyses of calcium-lead hydroxylapatites reveal that absorbed water on the surface of the crystals is lost within about 100° and thereafter within about 100° water trapped in the crystal interior along with water associated with phosphate anions is lost without formation of a definite hydrate. The kinetics were therefore studied thermogravimetrically and the energy of activation of the main steps of dehydroxylation were calculated by Freeman and Cor­rell method. The E values thus obtained were explained on theoretical ground.

 Thermogravimetric and differential thermal analyses of calcium-lead hydroxylapatites reveal that absorbed water on the surface of the crystals is lost within about 100° and thereafter within about 100° water trapped in the crystal interior along with water associated with phosphate anions is lost without formation of a definite hydrate. The kinetics were therefore studied thermogravimetrically and the energy of activation of the main steps of dehydroxylation were calculated by Freeman and Cor­rell method. The E values thus obtained were explained on theoretical ground.
